methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $240M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Xanadu Quantum Technologies, a Series C quantum computing company that has raised $240M+ to date, operates within a sector comprising 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Infleqtion, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and yields a $408M valuation. This approach applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $240M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, producing an implied post-money valuation of $408M. The Peer-Median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient granular comparables—while 7 same-sector peers exist, the filtered universe of same-stage and adjacent-stage quantum companies contains fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical confidence.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $347M to $469M, centered on a $408M median. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression midpoint. Key uncertainties include the scarcity of directly comparable Series C quantum computing financings, making reliance on stage-progression mechanics the primary driver. The valuation remains contingent on capital market conditions and the relative maturity of comparable-stage private quantum firms. Additional comparable transaction data or revenue/traction metrics would narrow the band materially.
